decisions.notes: At 18 lines and 32 words, this fictional webhook report keeps the scenario to one visible symptom and two short steps. The 14-line, 23-word quick start is an appropriately small Level 5 README fragment. Installation fence, two ordered usage steps, inline code, and a descriptive link are easy to locate; the Name formatter example uses original fictional vocabulary and does not overlap a published Goal.

CSV preview is a familiar, low-burden developer context, and all 23 words support the compact quick-start anatomy. The learner can identify the fence, numbered steps, two inline-code spans, and link at a glance; the JSON viewer example is distinct and appropriately partial.

This log-filter README remains a 14-line, 23-word syntax exercise, not a software tutorial. Its command fence, numbered usage, inline file names, and descriptive link are unambiguous, and the Image sizer example is original, short, and structurally helpful.

The release-note checker uses concise, idiomatic US English and stays at 14 lines and 23 words. Every block serves a Markdown decision, the Port checker example is unique, and the fictional commands, file names, and local links introduce no runtime AI, licensing, or external-data dependency.
